sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Topics 60

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Topics 60:1
items.html: (to the question) As to the matter of the Mochin that the Nukva takes from Ima "not through ZA [Zeir Anpin]" — the meaning is not that it is without the mediation of ZA, for that is inconceivable, that the Nukva should receive anything from her upper-upper, which is Ima, since no Partzuf can receive even the least thing from its upper-upper, but only from the upper one adjacent to it, in the discernment of cause and effect. What "not through ZA" means is: not of the essence of ZA. For the Mochin of Katnut the Nukva receives from the VAK [six ends] of ZA's Katnut, which are the essence of ZA, which will never be missing in him. But the Mochin of Gadlut she receives from the essence of Ima, which is of the discernment of the Masach [screen] of the Reshimot [records] of Nekudim, unfolding from the discernment of the Masach of SAG of AK [Adam Kadmon]. Whereas the Katnut of the Nukva she receives from the essence of ZA, which is from the Masach of the NHY of AK, of the discernment of the Vav and Nekuda [point], as is known. And this is what the Rav is precise to teach us: that the Mochin of Gadlut of the Nukva are no longer of the essence of ZA — that is, of the Reshimot of the Vav and Nekuda — but only of the discernment of the essence of AVI, as above. Yet she receives them by means of ZA, and not directly from Ima herself. And likewise the Rav taught us the same with ZA himself: his Katnut he receives from the NHY of AA [Arich Anpin], and it is the main essence of ZA; but the Gadlut, which for him is only a discernment of added goodness, he is already obliged to receive from AVI. And this too is for the above reason. (Item 109)
